seo.title: Voice & Speech AI implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping Voice & Speech AI projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist outlines the technical requirements for deploying robust Voice AI applications, covering audio pre-processing, latency management, transcription accuracy, and cost control.

items.title: Sample Rate Alignment
items.description: Verify that the audio capture sample rate matches the model requirements (typically 16kHz for STT) to avoid resampling artifacts.

items.title: Hardware Echo Cancellation
items.description: Ensure Acoustic Echo Cancellation (AEC) is enabled in the browser or OS to prevent the AI's output from being captured as new input.

items.title: Voice Activity Detection (VAD) Integration
items.description: Implement local VAD to stop transmission during silence, reducing unnecessary API costs and processing load.

items.title: Microphone Permission State Handling
items.description: Create explicit UI states for 'denied', 'blocked', and 'no device found' to guide users through hardware troubleshooting.

items.title: Input Gain Normalization
items.description: Apply a gain stage to normalize input levels, preventing clipping from loud speakers or inaudible low-volume inputs.

items.title: WebSocket/gRPC Streaming
items.description: Replace REST polling with full-duplex streaming protocols to minimize the overhead of repeated HTTP handshakes.

items.title: Partial Transcript Rendering
items.description: Display interim results to the user immediately rather than waiting for the final finalized transcript block.

items.title: Chunk Size Optimization
items.description: Tune audio buffer chunks (e.g., 100ms to 250ms) to find the balance between network overhead and processing speed.

items.title: TTS Audio Streaming
items.description: Configure the Text-to-Speech provider to stream audio bytes so playback begins before the entire sentence is synthesized.

items.title: Edge Deployment for Inference
items.description: Deploy STT/TTS models in regions closest to the end-user to minimize Round Trip Time (RTT).

items.title: Custom Vocabulary Injection
items.description: Supply a list of industry-specific terms, product names, or acronyms to the API to improve recognition of niche jargon.

items.title: Multi-Speaker Diarization
items.description: Enable speaker labels if the use case involves multiple participants to ensure correct turn-taking attribution.

items.title: Profanity and PII Filtering
items.description: Configure server-side filters to redact sensitive information or inappropriate language before data reaches the application layer.

items.title: Language Auto-Detection Verification
items.description: Test the system's ability to switch languages or handle code-switching if the target audience is multilingual.

items.title: Background Noise Stress Test
items.description: Validate WER (Word Error Rate) in environments with 60dB+ of ambient noise (e.g., street noise, office chatter).

items.title: SSML Implementation
items.description: Use Speech Synthesis Markup Language (SSML) to control prosody, emphasis, and pronunciation of specific terms.

items.title: Audio Format Selection
items.description: Use compressed formats like Opus or MP3 for delivery over mobile networks to reduce data consumption.

items.title: Playback Buffer Management
items.description: Implement a jitter buffer for synthesized audio to prevent gaps or 'pops' during network fluctuations.

items.title: Static Phrase Caching
items.description: Store pre-rendered audio files for common UI prompts (e.g., 'Hello', 'Goodbye') to eliminate API costs and latency.

items.title: Voice Fallback Logic
items.description: Define a secondary voice provider or a local Web Speech API fallback in case the primary TTS service fails.

items.title: WER Monitoring
items.description: Implement a pipeline to periodically compare system transcripts against human-verified ground truth to track accuracy over time.

items.title: API Credit Alerts
items.description: Set up automated alerts at 50%, 75%, and 90% of the monthly budget to prevent service suspension.

items.title: Connection Heartbeats
items.description: Implement ping/pong frames in WebSockets to detect and recover from silent network drops within 5 seconds.

items.title: Request Correlation IDs
items.description: Pass unique IDs through the entire audio pipeline (client -> STT -> LLM -> TTS) for debugging specific failed interactions.

items.title: User Feedback Loop
items.description: Provide a simple 'thumbs up/down' UI for transcription quality to identify edge cases where the model fails.
